_note: Worked example: Granite Point Mutual Insurance, a fictional regional P&C carrier writing personal auto and property. Demonstrates: policy book per household with effective dating, a quote->bound->renewed/cancelled policy machine, rate-table coverage premiums (rate x exposure units x tier factor), billing schedules DERIVED from bound policies with a written-vs-billed recon control, claims with lognormal long-tail severity and an FNOL->reserve->paid/denied machine, reserve transactions that develop over time (initial -> strengthening -> closing takedown), derived claim payments, the full layered warehouse stack (landing raw.* + staging stg.* per feed, canonical core.*, warehouse dims/facts, normalized nv_* views, business bv_* views, a materialized mv.* table, and competing business-unit mart_* views), a human-entered manual.line_of_business_mapping applied asymmetrically across actuarial vs distribution written-premium with an exact reconciliation, a workflow.case queue for mapping-request and exception review, the catalog.code_object code-object catalog with lineage and integration job-run history, and a loss-ratio mart that lands in the 55-75% band by line.

calendar._note: Monday FNOL peak (weekend losses reported late); spring hail / Q3 storm season in month weights.

loss_description: Rear-ended at a stoplight, bumper and trunk damage.
Backed into a parked vehicle in a lot.
Hail dents across hood and roof.
Tree limb fell on the garage roof.
Burst supply line under the kitchen sink.
Kitchen fire contained to cabinets and ceiling.
Theft of personal property from the vehicle.
Wind lifted shingles along the ridge line.
Slip and fall on the front walk, guest injured.
Sideswiped while merging on the interstate.
Water intrusion through flashing after storm.
Single-vehicle slide-off on ice, front end damage.
Dog bite to a visiting neighbor.
Sump pump failure flooded the finished basement.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables.name: line_of_business
tables.purpose: Personal lines written by the carrier; commission rates feed the policy-term fact.

tables._note: Filed rate table: 6 coverages per line, discrete base rates. Coverage premiums multiply these by exposure units and tier factor, so amount columns repeat exact values the way rated premium really does.
tables.name: coverage_type
tables.purpose: Coverage catalog with filed base rates and default limits.

columns.name: base_rate
columns._note: Per exposure unit: per vehicle on auto, per $150k dwelling band on property.

tables.name: agency
tables.purpose: Appointed independent agencies; production concentrates heavily in the top decile.

tables._note: PAS is the policyholder system of record. created_at is sorted with 62% pre-horizon backfill: a tenured book whose IDs correlate with onboarding date.
tables.name: policyholder
tables.purpose: Policyholder master in the policy administration system.

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns._note: The data window is 2024-2025, so a bare date gen cannot produce a 1950s DOB — store birth year instead.

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: sorted + backfill: 62% of the book predates the window, so in-window volume grows ~6%/yr instead of ramping from zero.

tables._note: CRM party is generated 1:1 per PAS policyholder with parent_copy so the two systems agree at birth; typo/conflict/late-arrival imperfections then make them disagree realistically.
tables.name: party
tables.purpose: CRM party master, synced nightly from the PAS.

tables._note: Policies enter the lifecycle machine at 'quoted'. Effective dating: effective_date lags the quote, expiration is a 12-month term. The small never-bound share deliberately leaves a few FNOLs failing coverage verification (DQ-004).
tables.name: policy
tables.purpose: Policy master: one row per quoted/issued personal-lines policy term.

rows._note: ~1.6 policies per household: auto+home bundles are common, a few holders run to 4-6.

columns.name: exposure_units
columns._note: Rating exposure: vehicles on auto, dwelling-value band (per $150k of Coverage A) on property — gives both lines a realistic premium spread.

columns.name: agency_id
columns._note: zipf 1.1: top-decile agencies carry over half of production, matching real agency concentration.

columns.name: effective_date
columns._note: Clamped at as-of so the claim loss window [effective, expiration] is always sampleable; the resulting cluster of year-end effective dates mirrors real new-year effective-date stacking.

tables.name: policy_event
tables.purpose: Policy status history emitted by the lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per policy per state entered

tables._note: Coverages assigned in canonical slot order per line (every auto policy carries BI+PD, every property policy carries Dwelling+Personal Property). Premium = filed rate x exposure units x tier factor: discrete, repeating amounts like real rated premium.
tables.name: policy_coverage
tables.purpose: Coverage-level rating detail per policy.
tables.grain: one row per policy per coverage

columns.name: is_endorsement
columns._note: ~14% of coverages are mid-term endorsements (added/changed after binding) rather than at-issue base coverage; distribution counts them at submission, actuarial only counts approved ones.

columns.name: endorsement_status
columns._note: Endorsements await UW sign-off: ~20% of endorsements sit pending/unapproved. Base coverages are not_applicable. Drives the asymmetric exclusion in mart_actuarial_written_premium.

tables._note: Claims attach to ~9% of policies over the window (~5-7%/yr frequency), scaled by line so each line's loss ratio lands in its target band. Severity is lognormal with a heavy bodily-injury/fire tail. loss_date is bounded by the policy term [effective_date, expiration_date]; claims on never-bound quotes feed the coverage-verification DQ queue, mirroring real coverage-mismatch-at-FNOL noise.
tables.name: claim
tables.purpose: Claims from FNOL through resolution.

rows._note: Continuous draw rounded to a count: ~10% of auto and ~8.7% of property policies see a claim, a few see 2-3. scale_by needs a CONTINUOUS distribution at low frequencies — round(1 x factor) swallows factors between 0.5 and 1.5 on integer draws.

columns.name: estimated_loss_amount
columns._note: Lognormal severity by claim type: bodily injury and fire carry the long tail.

columns.name: reported_date
columns._note: FNOL lag: median 2 days, tail to 40 (late-reported storm losses). business_days rolls weekend losses to Monday — the classic Monday FNOL peak.

tables.name: claim_event
tables.purpose: Claim status history emitted by the FNOL->reserve->paid/denied machine.
tables.grain: one row per claim per state entered

tables._note: Reserve change history is DERIVED from claim outcomes: initial round-hundred case reserve at reserve setup, mid-life strengthening on large claims, and a closing takedown that zeroes the case reserve on resolution. Sum(amount) per claim = current case reserve, >= 0, and 0 once closed.
tables.name: reserve_transaction
tables.purpose: Case reserve transactions per claim.
tables.grain: one row per claim per reserve change

tables.name: claim_payment
tables.purpose: Indemnity payments on paid claims; ~25% settle in two installments.

tables._note: Billing schedules are DERIVED from bound policies: full-pay/semi/quarterly/monthly installments that sum exactly to written premium (first installment absorbs rounding), plus pro-rata return-premium credits on cancellation. The written-vs-billed recon control ties to the cent except where installment restatements break it.
tables.name: installment
tables.purpose: Premium installment schedule per bound policy.
tables.grain: one row per policy per scheduled installment or credit

tables.name: payment
tables.purpose: Premium payments collected via the gateway; ~4% of due installments remain unpaid.

tables.name: recon_run
tables.purpose: Execution log for the finance reconciliation controls.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (live results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.3: claim reported over 30 days after loss
cases.4: claim on policy that never bound
cases.8: holder name mismatch PAS vs CRM
cases.9: mailing city mismatch PAS vs CRM
cases.12: policy bound via manual underwriting override
cases.13: coverage code missing approved LOB mapping

tables.name: case
tables.purpose: Exception and review queues (coverage verification, MDM, premium accounting, and the LOB mapping-request queue).

tables.name: case_event
tables.purpose: Case status history emitted by the case lifecycle machine.

tables._note: Job catalog for the code objects below. integration.job_run history references these; the procedures/function/extracts in catalog.code_object name them.
tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled job catalog (ETL feeds, refresh/recalc procedures, outbound extracts).

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Batch job run log; each run belongs to a catalog job, with occasional failures.

columns.name: error_note
columns._note: Non-null only when the run failed or ran partially.

tables._note: Human-entered mapping table. Actuarial maintains coverage-code -> line-of-business hierarchy node in a spreadsheet, uploaded here. Applied asymmetrically: mart_actuarial_written_premium joins it (left join + coalesce UNMAPPED), mart_distribution_written_premium uses the raw coverage code. 11 of 12 live coverage codes mapped (WTRBKP only has an unapproved row -> stays UNMAPPED); two retired-code rows; one conflicting COMP re-upload (last upload wins via row_number). Total-preserving: every coverage premium still counts, mapped or not, so the actuarial-vs-distribution difference is exactly the unapproved-endorsement exclusion.
tables.name: line_of_business_mapping
tables.purpose: Actuarial-maintained coverage-code to LOB-hierarchy-node mapping (spreadsheet upload).

columns.name: source_code
columns._note: coverage_code from ref.coverage_type. 11 live codes mapped; WTRBKP only appears on an unapproved row (id 13) so it stays UNMAPPED. COMP appears twice (ids 4 and 14: a conflicting re-upload, later upload wins). RIDESHARE/DRVASSIST/PETINJ are retired endorsement codes no longer rated.

columns.name: mapped_code
columns._note: LOB hierarchy node: AUTO.* and PROP.* leaves.

columns.name: approved_flag
columns._note: id 13 (WTRBKP) is the unapproved row that leaves WTRBKP UNMAPPED in the actuarial mart and feeds DQ-013 + the lob_mapping_request workflow case.

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations — raw -> stg -> xref -> core lineage, then warehouse dims/facts.
tables.name: pas_policyholder_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly PAS extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: policyholder
tables.purpose: Typed and cleaned policyholders; normalizes raw extract dates.

tables._note: Landing layer (rung 1), one raw.* table per source feed: verbatim text payloads with file/batch ids and ingested_at. format_drift corrupts the date columns post-derivation; the DQ views read these live and catch unparseable dates.
tables.name: cms_claim_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly claims-system FNOL/claim extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: claim
tables.purpose: Cleaned claims; typed, trimmed, case-guarded date parse, dedup against re-sent batches.

tables.name: billing_installment_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly billing installment-schedule extract as landed; runs after billing.installment so the rows exist.

tables.name: installment
tables.purpose: Cleaned billing installments; typed, case-guarded date parse, dedup.

tables.name: dist_agency_production_extract
tables.purpose: Monthly agency production file as landed (written premium booked by agency at submission); runs after core.policy.

tables.name: agency_production
tables.purpose: Cleaned agency production rows; typed, case-guarded date parse, dedup.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Source-system identifier crosswalk for canonical policyholders (PAS + CRM).

tables.name: policyholder
tables.purpose: Canonical policyholder joining PAS and CRM through the crosswalk.

tables.name: policy
tables.purpose: Canonical policy with written premium rolled up from coverage-level rating.

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_policyholder
tables.purpose: Policyholder dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_agency
tables.purpose: Agency dimension.

tables.name: dim_policy
tables.purpose: Policy dimension.

tables.name: fact_policy_term
tables.purpose: Policy-term fact with written premium and commission.
tables.grain: one row per bound policy term

tables.name: fact_premium_earned_monthly
tables.purpose: Monthly earned premium per bound policy, day-prorated from effective date to the earlier of expiration, cancellation, or as-of.
tables.grain: one row per bound policy per calendar month with earned exposure

tables.name: fact_claim_transaction
tables.purpose: Claim financial transactions: reserve changes and indemnity payments.
tables.grain: one row per claim financial transaction (reserve change or payment)

tables._note: Coverage-grain written-premium fact: one row per rated coverage on a bound policy, carrying the coverage code, the endorsement/approval flag (for the asymmetric exclusion), and agency/line/date keys. The competing actuarial-vs-distribution written-premium metric is built on this via nv_written_premium.
tables.name: fact_written_premium
tables.purpose: Coverage-level written premium for bound policy terms.
tables.grain: one row per coverage on a bound policy

tables._note: Materialized view (rung: mart). SQLite has no native matviews -> a mart table refreshed by insert-select from bv_loss_triangles. scale_exempt: ~8 quarters over the 2-year horizon regardless of multiplier. Paired with the rebuild_mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot code object and its job runs.
tables.name: quarter_end_reserve_snapshot
tables.purpose: Quarter-grain outstanding case-reserve snapshot, materialized from bv_loss_triangles.
tables.grain: one row per line per accident quarter

tables._note: Code-object catalog (rung: catalog). Self-registers every view from sqlite_master, plus authored target-platform source for the earned-premium procedure, reserve-recalc procedure, mv refresh procedure, pro-rata earning function, and the outbound bureau/reinsurance extracts.
tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed code objects (views, procedures, functions, extracts) with source text.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Lineage edges linking code objects to the tables they read and write.

state_machines.name: policy_lifecycle
state_machines._note: Quote -> bound -> in_force, then term-end renewal/lapse or midterm cancellation. Right-censored: policies quoted in late 2025 legitimately sit quoted/bound/in_force at the as-of date.

state_machines.name: claim_lifecycle
state_machines._note: FNOL -> open -> reserved -> paid/denied/closed-no-payment with long-tail settlement dwell (sigma 1.0). 7% of reserved claims legitimately sit open at as-of carrying case reserves.

state_machines.name: case_lifecycle
state_machines._note: Workflow queue lifecycle: open -> triaged -> in_review -> resolved, with an escalation branch. Right-censored: recent cases legitimately sit open/in_review at the as-of date.

derivations.name: raw_pas_policyholder_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the format_drift imperfection later pads a slice of these, which DQ-005 catches.
sql: select cast(policyholder_id as text), holder_code, holder_name, risk_tier, city, state, status,

derivations.name: stg_policyholder
sql: select cast(policyholder_id as integer), holder_code, trim(holder_name), lower(risk_tier), city, state, lower(status),

derivations.name: raw_cms_claim_extract
derivations._note: Claims feed landed verbatim as text with US-format dates; raw_claim_date_drift corrupts a slice of loss/reported dates post-derivation (DQ-014 catches them). ingested_at clamped at the as-of so no future timestamps.
sql: select cast(claim_id as text), claim_number, cast(policy_id as text), line, claim_type, status, fnol_channel,
printf('%.2f', estimated_loss_amount), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', loss_date), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', reported_date),

derivations.name: stg_claim
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL); row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
sql: select claim_id, claim_number, policy_id, line, claim_type, claim_status, fnol_channel, estimated_loss_amount, loss_date, reported_date, ingested_at
select cast(claim_id as integer) as claim_id, trim(claim_number) as claim_number,
cast(policy_id as integer) as policy_id, lower(line) as line, lower(claim_type) as claim_type,
lower(claim_status) as claim_status, lower(fnol_channel) as fnol_channel,
row_number() over (partition by cast(claim_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: Both PAS and CRM identifiers map to the canonical policyholder; missing_xref and stale_mapping imperfections later poke holes that DQ-006/DQ-007 catch.
sql: select row_number() over (order by p.policyholder_id), 'policyholder', p.policyholder_id,
'PolicyForge PAS', 'policyholder', cast(p.policyholder_id as text), date(p.created_at), null, 1
select 500000 + row_number() over (order by c.party_id), 'policyholder', c.pas_policyholder_id,
'Atrium CRM', 'party', cast(c.party_id as text), date(c.created_at), null, 1

derivations.name: core_policyholder
sql: select s.policyholder_id, s.holder_code, s.holder_name, s.risk_tier, s.city, s.state, s.status, s.created_date,
(select cast(x.source_record_id as integer) from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.policyholder_id and x.source_system = 'Atrium CRM'

derivations.name: core_policy
derivations._note: Written premium is the roll-up of coverage-level rated premium — the single source the billing schedule, the policy-term fact, and the recon control all hang off.
sql: select p.policy_id, p.policy_number, p.policyholder_id, p.agency_id, p.line, p.rating_tier,
p.exposure_units, p.payment_plan, p.status, p.quote_date, p.effective_date, p.expiration_date,
(select round(sum(c.coverage_premium), 2) from pas.policy_coverage c where c.policy_id = p.policy_id)

derivations.name: raw_dist_agency_production_extract
derivations._note: Agency production file landed verbatim from the derived core.policy rows (everything an agency booked at submission, including never-bound quotes); runs after core_policy. raw_agency_date_drift corrupts submitted dates post-derivation (DQ-016).
sql: select cast(p.policy_uid as text), p.policy_number, cast(p.agency_id as text), p.line, p.status,

derivations.name: stg_agency_production
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL); row_number dedup against re-sent files.
sql: select policy_id, policy_number, agency_id, line, policy_status, written_premium, submitted_date, ingested_at
select cast(policy_id as integer) as policy_id, trim(policy_number) as policy_number,
cast(agency_id as integer) as agency_id, lower(line) as line, lower(policy_status) as policy_status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(policy_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: billing_installment
derivations._note: Installment counts by plan (1/2/4/12); first installment absorbs rounding so the schedule sums to written premium exactly. Cancelled policies get a pro-rata return-premium credit.
sql: with recursive n(i) as (select 1 union all select i + 1 from n where i < 12),
select p.policy_uid as policy_id, p.effective_date, p.written_premium,
case p.payment_plan when 'full_pay' then 1 when 'semi_annual' then 2 when 'quarterly' then 4 else 12 end as n_inst,
case p.payment_plan when 'full_pay' then 0 when 'semi_annual' then 6 when 'quarterly' then 3 else 1 end as step_months,
case when p.payment_plan = 'monthly' then 6.0 else 0.0 end as fee
from core.policy p where p.status not in ('quoted', 'quote_declined')
case when n.i = 1 then round(pl.written_premium - (pl.n_inst - 1) * round(pl.written_premium / pl.n_inst, 2), 2)
from plan pl join n on n.i <= pl.n_inst;
where p.cancelled_date is not null and p.cancelled_date < p.expiration_date;

derivations.name: raw_billing_installment_extract
derivations._note: Billing installment-schedule feed landed verbatim from the derived billing.installment rows; runs after billing_installment. raw_installment_date_drift corrupts a slice of due dates post-derivation (DQ-015).
sql: select cast(installment_id as text), cast(policy_id as text), cast(installment_no as text), transaction_type,
printf('%.2f', installment_amount), printf('%.2f', fee_amount), strftime('%m/%d/%Y', due_date),

derivations.name: stg_installment
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL); row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
sql: select installment_id, policy_id, installment_no, transaction_type, installment_amount, fee_amount, due_date, ingested_at
select cast(installment_id as integer) as installment_id, cast(policy_id as integer) as policy_id,
cast(installment_no as integer) as installment_no, lower(transaction_type) as transaction_type,
cast(installment_amount as real) as installment_amount, cast(fee_amount as real) as fee_amount,
row_number() over (partition by cast(installment_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: billing_payment
derivations._note: Payments post 0-7 days after due date; ~4% of due installments stay unpaid (dunning fodder). Only installments due on or before the as-of date can have cash.
sql: case when i.installment_id % 10 < 5 then 'ach' when i.installment_id % 10 < 8 then 'card' else 'check' end,

derivations.name: cms_reserve_transaction
derivations._note: Reserve development: adjusters book a round-hundred initial reserve at 55-95% of the loss estimate, strengthen large claims at day 40, and take the case reserve to zero at resolution. Open claims keep a positive balance.
sql: from cms.claim c where c.reserved_at is not null;
select c.claim_id * 10 + 2, c.claim_id, c.line, 2, date(c.reserved_at, '+40 days'), 'reserve_strengthening',
- (select rt.amount from cms.reserve_transaction rt where rt.claim_id = c.claim_id and rt.txn_seq = 1)
where c.reserved_at is not null and c.estimated_loss_amount > 9000 and c.claim_id % 2 = 0
and date(c.reserved_at, '+40 days') < coalesce(date(c.paid_at), date(c.denied_at), date(c.closed_at), '2025-12-31');
-(select sum(rt.amount) from cms.reserve_transaction rt where rt.claim_id = c.claim_id)
where c.reserved_at is not null and coalesce(c.paid_at, c.denied_at, c.closed_at) is not null;

derivations.name: cms_claim_payment
derivations._note: Paid amount = estimate x 0.82-1.14 (id-derived, deterministic) — actuals drift from FNOL estimates. A quarter of settlements split 60/40 across two payments.
sql: select c.claim_id, c.line, date(c.paid_at) as pay_date,
from cms.claim c where c.paid_at is not null
case when claim_id % 5 = 0 then 'repair_network' when claim_id % 5 = 1 then 'claimant' else 'insured' end,
case when claim_id % 4 = 0 and date(pay_date, '+14 days') <= '2025-12-31'
select p.claim_id * 10 + 2, p.claim_id, p.line, 2, date(p.pay_date, '+14 days'), 'indemnity', 'insured',
from (select c.claim_id, c.line, date(c.paid_at) as pay_date,
from cms.claim c where c.paid_at is not null) p

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_policyholder
sql: select policyholder_uid, holder_name, risk_tier, city, state, status from core.policyholder;

derivations.name: wh_dim_agency
sql: select agency_id, agency_name, city, state, commission_tier, status from dist.agency;

derivations.name: wh_dim_policy
sql: select policy_uid, policy_number, line, rating_tier, payment_plan, status, effective_date, expiration_date

derivations.name: wh_fact_policy_term
sql: select p.policy_uid, p.policy_number, p.policyholder_id, p.agency_id, p.line, p.status, p.payment_plan,
from core.policy p join ref.line_of_business l on l.line_code = p.line

derivations.name: wh_fact_premium_earned_monthly
derivations._note: Day-prorated earning from effective date to the earliest of expiration, cancellation, or as-of. earned + unearned = written by construction.
sql: select date('2024-01-01') union all select date(month_start, '+1 month') from m where month_start < date('2025-12-01')
where p.status not in ('quoted', 'quote_declined') and p.effective_date < '2026-01-01'

derivations.name: wh_fact_claim_transaction
sql: select rt.reserve_txn_id, rt.claim_id, c.policy_id, rt.line, c.claim_type, c.loss_date,
from cms.reserve_transaction rt join cms.claim c on c.claim_id = rt.claim_id;
select 5000000 + cp.payment_id, cp.claim_id, c.policy_id, cp.line, c.claim_type, c.loss_date,
from cms.claim_payment cp join cms.claim c on c.claim_id = cp.claim_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_written_premium
derivations._note: Coverage-grain written premium for bound policy terms. policy_key/policyholder_key/agency_key are in the canonical id space (core.policy.policy_uid = dim_policy.policy_key). Carries the coverage code and endorsement/approval flags so the actuarial mart can exclude unapproved endorsements.
sql: select cov.coverage_id, p.policy_uid, p.policyholder_id, p.agency_id, p.line, cov.coverage_code,
join core.policy p on p.policy_uid = cov.policy_id

derivations.name: nv_written_premium
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): wide re-join of the coverage-grain written-premium fact to policy/agency dims with business column names. No aggregation. left joins because unmastered agencies/policies can legitimately lose a dim row.
sql: select f.coverage_id, f.policy_key, dp.policy_number, dp.rating_tier, dp.payment_plan, dp.status as policy_status,
f.agency_key, da.agency_name, da.state as agency_state, da.commission_tier,
f.line, f.coverage_code, f.effective_date, f.is_endorsement, f.endorsement_status, f.written_premium
left join wh.dim_policy dp on dp.policy_key = f.policy_key
left join wh.dim_agency da on da.agency_key = f.agency_key;

derivations.name: nv_premium_earned
derivations._note: Normalized view: monthly earned-premium fact re-joined to the policy dim with month attributes for the earned-premium business view.
sql: select e.policy_key, dp.policy_number, dp.rating_tier, dp.status as policy_status,
left join wh.dim_policy dp on dp.policy_key = e.policy_key;

derivations.name: nv_claim_transaction
derivations._note: Normalized view: claim financial-transaction fact with accident-period and development-age columns derived once, consumed by the loss-triangle business view.
sql: select t.fact_txn_id, t.claim_id, t.policy_id, t.line, t.claim_type, t.loss_date, t.txn_date, t.txn_type, t.amount,
strftime('%Y', t.loss_date) || '-Q' || ((cast(strftime('%m', t.loss_date) as integer) + 2) / 3) as accident_quarter,
cast((julianday(t.txn_date) - julianday(t.loss_date)) / 91.0 as integer) as dev_age_quarters

derivations.name: bv_earned_premium_monthly
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): monthly earned premium by line with a window running total of earned-to-date over the calendar — pro-rata earning rolled up via sum() over.
sql: select line, earn_month, min(month_start) as month_start,
from nv_premium_earned group by line, earn_month
select line, earn_month, month_start, earning_policies, earned_days, earned_premium,
round(sum(earned_premium) over (partition by line order by earn_month rows between unbounded preceding and current row), 2) as earned_premium_running_total

derivations.name: bv_loss_triangles
derivations._note: Business view: loss-development triangles by line x accident quarter x development age. lag() exposes the prior-age cumulative; the case ladder labels maturity. Cumulative incurred climbs toward ultimate as payments replace reserves.
sql: round(sum(case when txn_type = 'claim_payment' then amount else 0 end), 2) as incremental_paid
from nv_claim_transaction group by line, accident_quarter, dev_age_quarters
select line, accident_quarter, dev_age_quarters, claims_count, incremental_incurred, incremental_paid,
window w as (partition by line, accident_quarter order by dev_age_quarters rows between unbounded preceding and current row)
select line, accident_quarter, dev_age_quarters, claims_count, incremental_incurred, incremental_paid,
lag(cumulative_incurred) over (partition by line, accident_quarter order by dev_age_quarters) as prior_age_cumulative_incurred,

derivations.name: bv_agency_production
derivations._note: Business view: written premium and commission by agency with window rank/ntile so the top-decile production concentration is queryable.
sql: select f.agency_key, da.agency_name, da.state, da.commission_tier, da.status,
from wh.fact_policy_term f left join wh.dim_agency da on da.agency_key = f.agency_key
group by f.agency_key, da.agency_name, da.state, da.commission_tier, da.status
select agency_key, agency_name, state, commission_tier, status, bound_terms, written_premium, commission_amount,
rank() over (order by written_premium desc) as production_rank,
ntile(10) over (order by written_premium desc) as production_decile

derivations.name: mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot
derivations._note: Materialized view (rung: mart): insert-select into the mv.quarter_end_reserve_snapshot table from nv_claim_transaction, aggregated to line x accident quarter (cumulative incurred/paid at ultimate development). scale_exempt: ~8 quarters over the 2-year horizon. The rebuild_mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot code object refreshes it quarterly from bv_loss_triangles.
sql: round(sum(case when txn_type = 'claim_payment' then amount else 0 end), 2) as cumulative_paid,
round(sum(amount) - sum(case when txn_type = 'claim_payment' then amount else 0 end), 2) as outstanding_reserve

derivations.name: mart_actuarial_written_premium
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), ACTUARIAL. Applies the human-entered LOB mapping (left join + coalesce to UNMAPPED), dedups the conflicting COMP re-upload to the latest approved active row, and EXCLUDES unapproved endorsements (endorsement_status='pending'). Competes with mart_distribution_written_premium. Total-preserving on the mapping: every counted coverage rolls into a LOB node or the UNMAPPED bucket.
sql: row_number() over (partition by source_code order by uploaded_at desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
where approved_flag = 1 and (effective_end_date is null or effective_end_date > '2025-12-31')
left join mapping m on m.source_code = n.coverage_code and m.rn = 1

derivations.name: mart_distribution_written_premium
derivations._note: Business-unit view, DISTRIBUTION. Raw coverage code, NO LOB mapping, INCLUDES every coverage booked by the agency at submission (unapproved endorsements included). Legitimately disagrees with actuarial on the grand total by exactly the unapproved-endorsement amount.
sql: group by n.agency_key, n.agency_name, n.line, n.coverage_code;

derivations.name: mart_loss_ratio_by_line
derivations._note: Window-to-date loss ratio by line: earned premium vs paid + open case reserves. Built on the nv_ layer. Lands in the 55-75% band for both lines; accident-period detail lives in mart_reserve_development.
sql: select line, sum(earned_amount) as earned_premium, count(distinct policy_key) as earning_policies
select line, count(distinct claim_id) as claims_with_financials,
sum(case when txn_type = 'claim_payment' then amount else 0 end) as paid_losses,
sum(case when txn_type != 'claim_payment' then amount else 0 end) as case_reserves
from earned e left join losses l on l.line = e.line;

derivations.name: mart_reserve_development
derivations._note: Incurred development by line x accident quarter x development age, surfaced from bv_loss_triangles. Cumulative incurred climbs from initial reserves toward ultimate as payments replace reserves.

derivations.name: mart_agency_production
derivations._note: Agency production surfaced from bv_agency_production (ranked written premium).
sql: select agency_key, agency_name, state, commission_tier, status,

derivations.name: control_recon_written_vs_billed
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections: installment restatement reversals are the only source of breaks.
sql: select p.policy_uid, p.policy_number, p.line, p.status, p.written_premium,
group by policy_id) b on b.policy_id = p.policy_uid

derivations.name: control_recon_actuarial_distribution
derivations._note: Reconciles the competing written-premium metric. distribution_written_premium (everything booked, raw coverage code) minus actuarial_written_premium (unapproved endorsements excluded, LOB-mapped) must equal the unapproved-endorsement amount; unexplained_difference proves it (must be 0 because the LOB mapping is total-preserving). actuarial_unmapped quantifies the mapping coverage gap (the WTRBKP UNMAPPED bucket).
sql: with dist as (select round(sum(written_premium), 2) as total from nv_written_premium),
act as (select round(sum(written_premium), 2) as total from nv_written_premium
excluded as (select coalesce(round(sum(written_premium), 2), 0) as excluded_amount from nv_written_premium
and not exists (select 1 from manual.line_of_business_mapping m
from dist d, act a, excluded e, unmapped u;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'policyholders sharing an email' as issue, cast(p.policyholder_id as text) as entity_id
and exists (select 1 from pas.policyholder p2 where p2.email = p.email and p2.policyholder_id <> p.policyholder_id)
select 'DQ-002', 'claim references unknown policy', cast(c.claim_id as text)
from cms.claim c where not exists (select 1 from pas.policy p where p.policy_id = c.policy_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'claim reported over 30 days after loss', cast(c.claim_id as text)
from cms.claim c where julianday(c.reported_date) - julianday(c.loss_date) > 30
select 'DQ-004', 'claim on policy that never bound', cast(c.claim_id as text)
from cms.claim c join pas.policy p on p.policy_id = c.policy_id where p.bound_at is null
from raw.pas_policyholder_extract r where r.source_created_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-006', 'policyholder missing source mapping', cast(s.policyholder_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.policyholder_id and x.source_system = 'Atrium CRM')
or not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.policyholder_id and x.source_system = 'PolicyForge PAS')
select 'DQ-007', 'expired xref mapping still active', cast(x.entity_identifier_id as text)
from xref.entity_identifier x where x.active_flag = 1 and x.valid_to is not null and x.valid_to < '2025-12-31'
select 'DQ-008', 'holder name mismatch PAS vs CRM', cast(p.policyholder_id as text)
from pas.policyholder p join crm.party c on c.pas_policyholder_id = p.policyholder_id
select 'DQ-009', 'mailing city mismatch PAS vs CRM', cast(p.policyholder_id as text)
from pas.policyholder p join crm.party c on c.pas_policyholder_id = p.policyholder_id
select 'DQ-010', 'party feed latency breach', cast(c.party_id as text)
from crm.party c where julianday(c.ingested_at) - julianday(c.source_updated_at) > 5
from billing.payment b group by b.gateway_ref having count(*) > 1
select 'DQ-012', 'policy bound via manual underwriting override', cast(p.policy_id as text)
select 'DQ-013', 'coverage code missing approved LOB mapping', ct.coverage_code
where not exists (select 1 from manual.line_of_business_mapping m
from raw.cms_claim_extract r where r.loss_date_raw not like '__/__/____' or r.reported_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
from raw.billing_installment_extract r where r.due_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-016', 'unparseable raw agency production date', r.policy_id
from raw.dist_agency_production_extract r where r.submitted_date_raw not like '__/__/____';

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
derivations._note: Lineage edges for the authored procedures/function/extracts -> the tables they read and write.
sql: insert into catalog.lineage_edge (edge_id, code_object, operation, table_name, edge_note) values

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Authored target-platform source for the procedures/function/extracts, then self-registration of every deployed view from sqlite_master so the catalog provably matches the code. Runs LAST so all views exist. reserve_recalc is intentionally stale (references a renamed column) -- noted as known debt.
sql: insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes) values

('calc_earned_premium_monthly', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure calc_earned_premium_monthly() language sql as $$ delete from wh_fact_premium_earned_monthly; insert into wh_fact_premium_earned_monthly (policy_key, month_start, line, earned_days, earned_amount) select p.policy_uid, m.month_start, p.line, prorata_earned_factor(p.effective_date, p.expiration_date, m.month_start) as earned_days, round(p.written_premium * prorata_earned_factor(p.effective_date, p.expiration_date, m.month_start) / (julianday(p.expiration_date) - julianday(p.effective_date)), 2) from core_policy p cross join calendar_month m where p.status not in (''quoted'', ''quote_declined''); $$;', 'actuarial_systems', '2024-01-15 09:00:00', '2025-02-03 11:20:00', 'Monthly earned-premium build; scheduled by integration.job calc_earned_premium_monthly.'),

('reserve_recalc', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure reserve_recalc(as_of date) language plpgsql as $$ begin insert into cms_reserve_transaction (claim_id, line, txn_type, amount) select c.claim_id, c.line, ''reserve_strengthening'', round(c.estimated_loss_amount * 1.12, 0) - c.case_reserve_balance from cms_claim c where c.status = ''reserved'' and c.estimated_loss_amount > 9000; end; $$;', 'claims_finance', '2024-01-20 08:30:00', '2025-01-31 17:45:00', 'KNOWN DEBT: references retired column cms_claim.case_reserve_balance (now derived from reserve_transaction); flagged for cleanup.'),

('rebuild_mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure rebuild_mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot() language sql as $$ delete from mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot; insert into mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot (line, accident_quarter, claims_count, cumulative_incurred, cumulative_paid, outstanding_reserve) select line, accident_quarter, claims_count, cumulative_incurred, cumulative_paid, round(cumulative_incurred - cumulative_paid, 2) from bv_loss_triangles t where t.dev_age_quarters = (select max(dev_age_quarters) from bv_loss_triangles t2 where t2.line = t.line and t2.accident_quarter = t.accident_quarter); $$;', 'actuarial_systems', '2024-02-01 09:00:00', '2025-04-10 09:15:00', 'Quarterly reserve-snapshot refresh; scheduled by integration.job rebuild_mv_quarter_end_reserve_snapshot.'),

('prorata_earned_factor', 'function', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace function prorata_earned_factor(eff date, exp date, month_start date) returns integer language sql as $$ select cast(min(julianday(date(month_start, ''+1 month'')), julianday(exp)) - max(julianday(month_start), julianday(eff)) as integer); $$;', 'actuarial_systems', '2023-11-02 10:00:00', '2024-06-15 10:00:00', 'Day-count of a policy term earned within a calendar month; used by calc_earned_premium_monthly.'),

('nightly_bureau_stat_report', 'extract', 'sql', 'select f.policy_key, f.coverage_code, f.line, f.effective_date, round(f.written_premium, 2) as stat_premium from wh_fact_written_premium f where f.effective_date >= date(''now'', ''start of year''); -- format: fixed-width; destination: sftp://stat-bureau.example.org/in/; schedule: 0 23 * * *', 'regulatory_reporting', '2024-03-01 09:00:00', '2025-04-10 09:15:00', 'Outbound nightly statistical premium report to the bureau (job nightly_bureau_stat_report).'),

('reinsurance_bordereau_extract', 'extract', 'sql', 'select c.claim_number, c.line, c.loss_date, round(sum(case when t.txn_type = ''claim_payment'' then t.amount else 0 end), 2) as ceded_paid, round(sum(case when t.txn_type != ''claim_payment'' then t.amount else 0 end), 2) as ceded_reserve from wh_fact_claim_transaction t join cms_claim c on c.claim_id = t.claim_id where c.estimated_loss_amount > 25000 group by 1,2,3; -- format: CSV; destination: sftp://reinsurer.example.com/bordereau/; schedule: 0 6 1 * *', 'reinsurance_accounting', '2024-04-01 09:00:00', '2025-05-12 09:15:00', 'Monthly excess-of-loss bordereau to the reinsurer (job reinsurance_bordereau_extract).');
insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes)
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';

imperfections.name: holder_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Hand-keyed names at quote accumulate typos; CRM keeps the clean sync copy, so DQ-008 sees the mismatch.

imperfections.name: duplicate_policyholders
imperfections.scenario: Agents re-key existing insureds at quote; shared emails surface in DQ-001 for the MDM queue.

imperfections.name: crm_city_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: Address updated in one system only; DQ-009 feeds the returned-mail review queue.

imperfections.name: crm_feed_late_arrivals
imperfections.scenario: Nightly PAS-to-CRM sync misses runs; DQ-010 flags latency breaches.

imperfections.name: claim_orphan_policies
imperfections.scenario: Out-of-sequence policy rewrites hard-delete terms claims still reference; DQ-002 routes them to coverage verification.

imperfections.name: uw_manual_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Underwriters override system declines/rates with supervisor approval; DQ-012 lists them for UW audit.

imperfections.name: raw_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-005 catches what staging cannot parse.

imperfections.name: missing_crm_mappings
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost in the 2024 CRM migration; DQ-006 shows unmapped policyholders.

imperfections.name: stale_xref_mappings
imperfections.scenario: Expired mappings never re-pointed; DQ-007 flags active rows past their valid_to.

imperfections.name: installment_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Billing corrections issued as reversal + restated pairs; these are the only breaks in the written-vs-billed recon.

imperfections.name: payment_webhook_dups
imperfections.scenario: Payment gateway retries post the same settlement twice; DQ-011 spots repeated gateway references.

imperfections.name: raw_claim_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy claims extract pads dates differently after a patch; DQ-014 catches the unparseable ones.

imperfections.name: raw_installment_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Billing extract date format drifts after a patch; DQ-015 catches it.

imperfections.name: raw_agency_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Agency production file date format drifts after a patch; DQ-016 catches it.

imperfections.name: duplicate_job_runs
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate run records.

controls.rule_name: Written premium ties to billed installments
Case reserve equals reserve transaction sum and zeroes at closure
Gateway cash applied to installments due
Actuarial written premium reconciles to distribution booked premium
controls.expected_break_rate: difference equals unapproved-endorsement premium; unexplained_difference must be 0

dq_rules.condition: no two policyholders share an email
claim reported within 30 days of loss
every policyholder mapped in both PAS and CRM
holder name agrees across PAS and CRM
mailing city agrees across PAS and CRM
ingested within 5 days of source update
manual underwriting overrides carry secondary approval
every live coverage code has an approved LOB mapping

dataflows.name: policyholder_mastering
dataflows.sequence: pas.policyholder -> raw.pas_policyholder_extract -> stg.policyholder -> xref -> core.policyholder -> wh.dim_policyholder

dataflows.name: quote_to_bill
dataflows.sequence: pas.policy -> policy_lifecycle machine -> core.policy (written premium) -> billing.installment -> billing.payment -> recon CTL-001

dataflows.name: claims_lifecycle
dataflows.sequence: cms.claim (FNOL) -> claim_lifecycle machine -> cms.reserve_transaction + cms.claim_payment -> wh.fact_claim_transaction -> loss ratio / development marts

dataflows.name: view_stack
dataflows.sequence: wh facts/dims -> nv_written_premium / nv_premium_earned / nv_claim_transaction -> bv_earned_premium_monthly / bv_loss_triangles / bv_agency_production -> mv.quarter_end_reserve_snapshot / mart_actuarial_written_premium / mart_distribution_written_premium / mart_loss_ratio_by_line / mart_reserve_development / mart_agency_production

dataflows.name: written_premium_competing_metric
dataflows.sequence: wh.fact_written_premium -> nv_written_premium -> mart_actuarial_written_premium (mapping applied, unapproved endorsements excluded) vs mart_distribution_written_premium (raw, everything booked) -> control_recon_actuarial_distribution + DQ-013 + workflow lob_mapping_request

dataflows.name: code_catalog
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + authored procs/function/extracts -> catalog.code_object; catalog.lineage_edge; integration.job -> integration.job_run
